title: [v3.2.3-rhel] Backports for health check files
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

This backports https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/12742 to `v3.4.3-rhel`.

Jira: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-24871

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
To comment on the supportedness error,

```
$GOSRC/$SCRIPT_BASE/setup_environment.sh
************************************************
ERROR: As of 20250531 this branch is probably
no longer supported in RHEL 8.4.z, please
confirm this with RHEL PM.  If so:

It should be removed from Cirrus-Cron,
the .cirrus.yml file removed, and
the VM images (manually) unmarked
'permanent=true'  (contrib/cirrus/setup_environment.sh:31 in main())
************************************************
```

This stream in RHEL 8.4 isn't supported in the sense of being updated for or consumable by RHEL customers, but it is still consumed by RHOSP 17.1, which is where this request came from.

> $GOSRC/$SCRIPT_BASE/setup_environment.sh
> ************************************************
> ERROR: As of 20250531 this branch is probably
> no longer supported in RHEL 8.4.z, please
> confirm this with RHEL PM.  If so:
> 
> This stream in RHEL 8.4 isn't supported in the sense of being updated for or consumable by RHEL customers, but it is still consumed by RHOSP 17.1, which is where this request came from.

@dashea please feel free to update the date and comment in that file.  When I originally added it, I had no notion about podman being used in other products.  In case it helps, I found an e-mail chain suggesting: "RHOSP 17.1 and 16.2 [support] both run until mid-2027".

Also, don't expect much from CI on this branch.  I just checked and see it's only doing a very simple build + lint type checks. It's probably worth doing some manual testing to confirm the health checks are behaving as expected.
I thought this branch was long since dead and buried, but LGTM for what it's worth

> I thought this branch was long since dead and buried, but LGTM for what it's worth

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/54344c4d-d3fd-48ff-b2a7-01d4df50c939)
Yeah, unfortunately, OSP has a dependency on this branch, and will for a while.  It still breathes @mheon

@dashea it looks like a test script might need to be told that this still needs support.

```
$GOSRC/$SCRIPT_BASE/setup_environment.sh
************************************************
ERROR: As of 20250531 this branch is probably
no longer supported in RHEL 8.4.z, please
confirm this with RHEL PM.  If so:

It should be removed from Cirrus-Cron,
the .cirrus.yml file removed, and
the VM images (manually) unmarked
'permanent=true'  (contrib/cirrus/setup_environment.sh:31 in main())
************************************************
```
Reset the EOL date based on https://access.redhat.com/support/policy/updates/openstack/platform

LGTM
and happy green test buttons.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
